report_name: Emerging Ransomware Group 'Mnt6' Claims Cyberattack on New Zealand Electrical Firm McKay

threat_actors.name: Mnt6
threat_actors.description: A new ransomware group that surfaced in late April 2026, claiming responsibility for an attack on New Zealand contractor McKay. It operates a darknet leak site and employs a double-extortion model.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data exfiltration and the threat of publication.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The Mnt6 group exfiltrated data from McKay as part of its double-extortion model, threatening to release the data publicly on its leak site.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: This technique is the primary tactic of the Mnt6 ransomware group, as defined by their double-extortion model of operation.

malware.name: Mnt6 Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Mnt6 group. Its specific characteristics are unknown, but it is used in double-extortion attacks.

identities.name: McKay
identities.description: A major New Zealand-based electrical contractor targeted by the Mnt6 ransomware group in January 2026.

infrastructure.name: Mnt6 darknet leak site
infrastructure.description: A darknet site used by the Mnt6 group to list victims and threaten the publication of stolen data.

indicators.name: Potential Data Exfiltration using Rclone
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Data Exfiltration using Rclone
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e6f-4a3b-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Monitors for the use of rclone.exe, a legitimate data transfer tool often abused by attackers for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.cyberdaily.au/security/10777-exclusive-kiwi-electrical-contractor-confirms-cyber-attack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of rclone by administrators or backup scripts.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rclone.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: A hunting hint from the article suggests monitoring for the use of legitimate data transfer tools like rclone, which are often abused by attackers for data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Lateral Movement Attempt via Net Use
indicators.pattern:
title: Lateral Movement Attempt via Net Use
id: 8c7d9e0f-1a2b-4c5d-6e7f-8a9b0c1d2e3f
status: experimental
description: Detects lateral movement attempts using 'net use' to map administrative shares (C$, ADMIN$).
references:
  - https://www.cyberdaily.au/security/10777-exclusive-kiwi-electrical-contractor-confirms-cyber-attack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_base:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'use'
  selection_share:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'C$'
      - 'ADMIN$'
  condition: selection_base and selection_share
indicators.description: A hunting hint from the article suggests looking for lateral movement attempts using `net use` to map administrative shares.

relationships.source_name: Lateral Movement Attempt via Net Use
